title: Multiply difference of squares
description: Multiply differences of squares similar to the form (x+a)(x-a).

hints:
Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a-b)$

This form expands to what we call "a difference of squares":

$(\blueE a+\maroonE b)(\blueE a-\maroonE b)=\blueE a^2-\maroonE b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
(\blueE x+\maroonE 3)(\blueE x-\maroonE 3)&=\blueE x^2-\maroonE 3^2
\\\\
&=x^2-9
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a-b)$

This form expands to what we call "a difference of squares":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)(\blueD a-\maroonC b)=\blueD a^2-\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
(\blueD x+\maroonC 5)(\blueD x-\maroonC 5)&=\blueD x^2-\maroonC 5^2
\\\\
&=x^2-25
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a-b)$

This form expands to what we call "a difference of squares":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)(\blueD a-\maroonC b)=\blueD a^2-\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
(\blueD x+\maroonC 8)(\blueD x-\maroonC 8)&=\blueD x^2-\maroonC 8^2
\\\\
&=x^2-64
\end{align}$

Notice that this expression has the following special form:

$(a+b)(a-b)$

This form expands to what we call "a difference of squares":

$(\blueD a+\maroonC b)(\blueD a-\maroonC b)=\blueD a^2-\maroonC b^2$

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
(\blueD 4+\maroonC x)(\blueD 4-\maroonC x)&=\blueD 4^2-\maroonC x^2
\\\\
&=16-x^2

$(a+b)(a-b)$

This form expands to what we call "a difference of squares":

\\\\

This form expands to what we call "a difference of squares":

Using the above pattern, we get:

$\begin{align}
(\blueD 2+\maroonC x)(\blueD 2-\maroonC x)&=\blueD 2^2-\maroonC x^2
\\\\

(\blueD{2x}+\maroonC 5)(\blueD{2x}-\maroonC 5)&=(\blueD{2x})^2-\maroonC 5^2
